execution_recommendation.reason: Packaging, architecture, mirror export, and tier boundary work span multiple modules with non-trivial dependencies and release implications.

tasks.title: Create tier capability map
tasks.description: Classify every current tool, workflow, and product-relevant contract into free, advanced, or studio_future so the product boundary becomes explicit and enforceable.
acceptanceCriteria: A documented capability matrix exists for all user-facing MCP tools and major workflow surfaces.
Every classified item is labeled as free, advanced, or studio_future.
The matrix identifies ambiguous boundary cases and proposed resolution rules.
No current public-facing tool remains unclassified.

tasks.title: Introduce tier and entitlement contracts
tasks.description: Add shared product-tier and entitlement contracts that can classify tools, modules, and future providers without hard-coding Studio into current Free or Advanced paths.
acceptanceCriteria: Shared contracts define product tiers including free, advanced, and studio_future.
The contracts are exported through the shared package and build successfully.
Type-level tests cover the new contracts.
The contract design allows future Studio introduction without renaming current Free or Advanced tiers.

tasks.title: Carve public core and private module seams
tasks.description: Create a module strategy where Free-safe code can build independently while private Advanced modules remain additive and separable.
acceptanceCriteria: A documented module boundary exists for public core, private advanced modules, and studio seams.
No Free-safe module imports an Advanced-only implementation directly.
A baseline import rule or verification mechanism is defined for public versus private boundaries.
The proposed structure preserves current runtime behavior before product gating is applied.

tasks.title: Define and document the Free product surface
tasks.description: Translate the current repo capability surface into a coherent public Free offer covering assessment, recommendations, creator light, simple assembly, local library usage, and validation-safe workflows.
acceptanceCriteria: A Free product surface document exists and matches the agreed launch strategy.
The Free surface includes local Elementor Library as the primary target system.
The Free surface excludes premium library and cloud-library promises.
The Free surface maps to currently existing or intentionally planned repo capabilities.

tasks.title: Define and document the Advanced product surface
tasks.description: Package the deeper workflow layer as a private Advanced surface including deeper wizard flows, stronger recommendations, brand adaptation, output critique, and premium library access.
acceptanceCriteria: An Advanced product surface document exists and is distinct from Free.
Advanced is described as a productivity and workflow upgrade rather than Free plus minor extras.
The Elementify Library is clearly described as a curated premium library, not a cloud library.
No Advanced requirement introduces a mandatory Studio dependency.

tasks.title: Create library provider boundaries
tasks.description: Separate local Elementor Library behavior, curated premium library behavior, and future cloud library behavior behind explicit provider-ready boundaries.
acceptanceCriteria: The architecture distinguishes local, premium, and cloud library roles.
Advanced premium library logic remains local-site operational and does not imply cloud sync.
A future cloud library seam exists without introducing a runtime dependency today.
The distinction is documented in architecture notes and reflected in contracts or provider interfaces.

tasks.title: Implement Free-safe tool registration and gating
tasks.description: Ensure the public Free build can register and expose only Free-safe tools while remaining fully functional on current local-site workflows.
acceptanceCriteria: Tool registration can be limited to Free-safe surfaces without runtime errors.
Assessment, recommendations, creator-light, local-library flows, and validation remain available in the Free path.
No Advanced-only tool is required for Free startup or execution.
Tests confirm the Free registration surface is self-consistent.

tasks.title: Wire Advanced-only workflow modules
tasks.description: Attach private Advanced workflows to the tier model so deeper flows can build on the shared core without leaking into the public mirror.
acceptanceCriteria: Brand adaptation and output critique are wired as Advanced-only product surfaces.
Advanced-only recommendation or creator depth is clearly gated behind the tier model.
Free builds remain green when Advanced modules are excluded.
Private module boundaries are documented and testable.

tasks.title: Define Forgejo primary and GitHub mirror export rules
tasks.description: Document and implement the release rules by which the private Forgejo primary remains complete while the GitHub mirror exports only the Free product surface.
acceptanceCriteria: A documented mirror export process exists for Forgejo primary to GitHub mirror.
The export process excludes private Advanced modules and premium assets.
A verification step detects forbidden imports or references before mirror publication.
The release rules do not rely on manual file-by-file cleanup.

tasks.title: Validate Studio seams without shipping Studio
tasks.description: Confirm that future Studio concepts can be introduced through providers and entitlements later, without turning current Free or Advanced code into pseudo-cloud implementations.
acceptanceCriteria: Studio future seams are documented for cloud library, cross-site reuse, and delivery orchestration.
No current Free or Advanced feature claims Studio cloud behavior prematurely.
The entitlement model can introduce Studio without breaking existing tier names or packaging.
A short architecture note explains what remains intentionally deferred.
